articles.title: Aftershock frays nerves as many Venezuelans left to fend for themselves
articles.summary: In devastated areas people are using crowbars, pickaxes and their bare hands to try to reach survivors.

articles.title: Mum rescued from Venezuela rubble with newborn baby tells BBC how he helped her survive
articles.summary: The "miracle" rescue of 18-day-old baby Juan David is a symbol of hope in Venezuela after the devastating earthquakes.

articles.title: Six people shot dead at centre for mothers and children in Germany
articles.summary: The male suspect who has been arrested was in a custody dispute over his baby daughter, police say.

articles.title: Trump's final appeal of E Jean Carroll sex abuse case rejected
articles.summary: The Supreme Court decision means the US president will now have to pay Carroll the $5m (£3.6m) in damages she had been awarded.

articles.title: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
articles.summary: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

articles.title: Putin makes rare admission of fuel shortages caused by Ukrainian strikes
articles.summary: The Russian president acknowledged Ukraine's attacks were "obviously creating problems" but denied the shortages were "critical".

articles.title: South African leader warns anti-migrant protesters ahead of unofficial deadline
articles.summary: Thousands of people from other African countries have left South Africa ahead of Tuesday's deadline set by anti-migrant groups.

articles.title: Pakistani strikes kill at least 28 civilians in Afghanistan, UN says
articles.summary: Pakistan claims it was targeting militants near its border, but the Taliban government calls it an "atrocity".

articles.title: DR Congo bans mass gatherings in the capital to prevent spread of Ebola
articles.summary: Opposition politicians accuse the government of using the outbreak to halt a planned protest.

articles.title: Supreme Court blocks Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook
articles.summary: The decision, seen as a win for central bank independence, sends the fight over removal back to the lower courts.
